page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with 128.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The hardware configuration includes 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate solid multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48,576.94 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,490.0, both falling in the average range. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 941.0 and a weak gzip compression throughput of 20.29 GB/sec. However, the system demonstrates robust data handling, achieving 38.19 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and an average Passmark database operations score of 20,399.25. Memory bandwidth is also notable, reaching 1,893.97 GB/sec for cached small block reads.

The im4gn.8xlarge offers high storage density and network throughput, making it highly cost-efficient for storage-intensive workloads compared to general-purpose instances. However, the ARM64 architecture lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, which limits its performance in advanced vector mathematics and heavy machine learning tasks, as evidenced by weak LLM inference speeds. This server is best suited for scale-out databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and high-throughput storage applications that benefit from massive local NVMe capacity and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. While single-core CPU performance and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak, the instance delivers solid multi-core and database performance, achieving 38.19 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Lacking specialized x86 instruction sets like AVX, it trades raw compute acceleration for high-density local storage and efficient memory bandwidth. This configuration is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, and high-throughput storage workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server with 32 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized server featuring fifteen terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage and fifty gigabits per second network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
128.0 GB of system memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong database performance with 38.19 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for scale-out databases, caching layers, and high-throughput storage
